source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A symmetrical square wave of 50% duty cycle has amplitude of \(\pm 15 \, \text{V}\) and time period of \(0.4\pi \, \text{ms}\). This square wave is applied across a series RLC circuit with \(R=5 \, \Omega\), \(L=10 \, \text{mH}\), and \(C=4 \, \mu\text{F}\). The amplitude of the 5000 \(\text{rad/s}\) component of the capacitor voltage (in Volt) is ______.
extraction_notes: Removed leading answer-format hint from problem text.
Image field is a PIL image object placeholder, not a path/URL.

requires circuit analysis of a series RLC to obtain the capacitor-voltage response at that angular frequency
involves combining source-spectrum reasoning with frequency-domain transfer calculation
question is numeric and not multiple-choice, so the result must be derived rather than selected
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit value: 192.0
all circuit parameters and waveform parameters are specified in the question text
